category_title: Preschoolers with frequent inappropriate social behaviors (yelling, taking toys, crying)
minimal_context: Gregory is participating in free play time with his peers in the preschool classroom.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is time for free play. Go pick a center and play with the other children.

STUDENT: (Walks over to a peer) I want those! (Takes the other child's toys)

TUTOR: Let's try to play together.

STUDENT: (Hides the toys behind his back) No!

TUTOR: Remember to use your words.

STUDENT: (Yells at the other child and starts crying) They are mine!

options.A: Remain available without forcing interaction during free play and other routines, first simply sitting near Gregory and then speaking quietly and gently touching his shoulder.
options.B: Establish a classroom norm where disagreement is acceptable and provide a platform for an evidence-based exchange of viewpoints among the children during free play and other routines.
options.C: Review the Thumbs Up sheet, have Gregory count all of the thumbs up and thumbs down that are circled, and talk to him about whether or not he had an overall thumbs-up or thumbs-down day.
options.D: Act persistently without becoming angry or insulting him in public, ensuring Gregory gets neither an irritated response nor an accusation, but instead remaining very patient and restrained.
